description: Proposal to modernize public cryptography teaching examples away from abstract Alice/Bob actors toward family, household, school, civic, and community actors with clearer consent, trust, guardianship, and replay boundaries.

target_context.public_docs_surface: NSA.gov and adjacent public cybersecurity education materials

root_rule: No more Alice and Bob as the only public teaching model. Upgrade examples to real-world human trust contexts without exposing private people.
why_change: Alice and Bob are useful but too abstract for families, schools, elders, children, and civic users.
Modern security failures often happen at the relationship layer, not just the protocol layer.
Families need examples that explain consent, identity, device trust, recovery, guardianship, scams, and public/private boundaries.
Real public education should teach people how cryptography protects relationships, memories, accounts, and communities.

example_upgrade_map.old_example: Alice sends Bob an encrypted message.
example_upgrade_map.upgraded_example: A parent sends a school form to a teacher. The child’s private details stay encrypted, the receipt proves delivery, and the school cannot silently alter the record.
A scammer intercepts a family account recovery link. The system detects the device mismatch and blocks credential theft.
A public record is changed after filing. Hash history exposes the change and creates a replayable challenge path.
A caregiver verifies that a medication instruction came from the right clinic before acting on it.

example_upgrade_map.old_example: Alice and Bob share a secret key.
example_upgrade_map.upgraded_example: A family sets up recovery keys so one lost phone does not erase the family archive or expose private memories.

mrs_wisdom_layer.role: Translate cryptographic trust into human communication, consent, recovery, and reputation boundaries.
mrs_wisdom_layer.md_output: Human-readable guides, stories, examples, family safety scripts, and education pages.
mrs_wisdom_layer.json_output: Machine-readable actor models, receipts, threat models, replay paths, and validation fixtures.

mr_wisdom_layer.role: Represent cryptographic structure, receipts, schemas, hashes, state machines, and deterministic replay.
mr_wisdom_layer.home: JSONWisdom repo stack and Base identity surfaces

education_doc_template.title: Family Cryptography Actors: Beyond Alice and Bob
sections: Why Alice and Bob are not enough
